title: Drift-guard hardcodes the canonical registry path and never globs other parent skills, so C-plus parity is enforced for deep-loop-workflows ONLY
evidence: Line 32 `resolve(repoRoot, '.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json')` is the sole registry read (line 45); every assertion (lines 80-109) projects from that one file. No discovery of any other `*/mode-registry.json`.
why: VERDICT: the reusability standard is self-enforcing for the reference instance only, not for the next parent skill. A 2nd parent's registry is invisible to this test, so its lexical modes can ship un-wired and stay green — the sold 'maps==registry' C-plus guarantee holds at N=1 and is false at N=2.
fix: Generalize the test to discover every `*/mode-registry.json` under `.opencode/skills/` and assert per-skill parity, OR make /create:sk-skill-parent scaffold a required, CI-run per-skill drift-guard + advisor-map wiring.

title: 4c downgrades a non-canonical inert lexical mode to WARN-only (exit 0) while the canonical path FAILs (exit 1)
evidence: Canonical branch (332-339) `fail()` -> fails++ (85) -> return 1 (361). Non-canonical branch (341-348) `warn()` at 346 -> warns++ only (89); main() returns 0 (357-359). A declared lexical mode whose legacyAdvisorId is absent from the advisor map passes for any non-canonical skill.
why: Confirms 'ship a lexical mode that never routes and nothing fails' — the structural checker green-lights an unroutable mode in a second parent skill.
fix: For non-canonical targets treat inert lexical modes as FAIL (or require an accompanying per-skill drift-guard test to exist), not WARN.

title: CI gate only triggers on the canonical registry path and audits only the canonical default target
evidence: paths (10-12) lists only `deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json`, advisor `mcp_server/**`, and the checker script — a new parent skill's registry matches none, so edits never trigger the workflow. On trigger, line 43 runs the checker with no arg -> DEFAULT_TARGET canonical (cjs 67/124), and the vitest run list (38-41) is three hardcoded canonical tests.
why: Three independent layers all scope to N=1; the 'authoritative CI gate' (header lines 3-5) never covers a second parent skill, so the recommended per-skill drift-guard would not even be executed by this workflow.
fix: Add new parent-skill registries to paths, run parent-skill-check against each parent dir, and add each per-skill drift-guard to the vitest run list — ideally auto-emitted by /create:sk-skill-parent.

file: .opencode/commands/README.txt + speckit/README.txt + install_guides/README.md + SET-UP - AGENTS.md + references/workflows/quick_reference.md + auto_mode_contract.md + speckit/assets/speckit_plan_presentation.txt
line: README.txt:38,147-151,211; speckit/README.txt:60,61,125,146,148,149,152,191; README.md:1338,1341; SET-UP - AGENTS.md:602; quick_reference.md:107,108; auto_mode_contract.md:184; speckit_plan_presentation.txt:155
title: Confirmed 28 stale /deep:start-*-loop and ask-ai-council command tokens in user-facing docs
evidence: rg over the in-scope paths (specs/ + node_modules excluded) yields 24 `/deep:start-*` occurrences + 4 `ask-ai-council` = 28 total; e.g. README.txt:150 `/deep:start-research-loop <topic>`, README.txt:147 `/deep:ask-ai-council`, README.md:1338 lists `/deep:start-research-loop`, `/deep:start-review-loop`.
why: Current command surface was renamed to /deep:research, /deep:review, /deep:agent-improvement, /deep:model-benchmark, /deep:ai-council (deep/ dir + skills list); the documented slash forms no longer resolve, so users following these docs invoke non-existent commands.
fix: Phase-008 docs sweep: replace each /deep:start-<x>-loop token with its renamed /deep:<x> form and /deep:ask-ai-council with /deep:ai-council across the 7 files.

title: Five stale tokens name command files/YAML that no longer exist on disk
evidence: speckit/README.txt:102 `start-research-loop.md`, :103 `start-review-loop.md`, :104 `ask-ai-council.md`, :110 `deep_ask-ai-council_auto.yaml`; README.txt:85 `ask-ai-council.md`. `find .opencode/commands -name start-*loop*.md` and `-name ask-ai-council*` return nothing; deep/assets/ contains `deep_ai-council_auto.yaml`, not `deep_ask-ai-council_auto.yaml`.
why: These docs draw a directory tree / file map pointing at physically-deleted files (renamed to deep/research.md, review.md, ai-council.md, deep_ai-council_auto.yaml), so the doc's structural map is factually wrong, not merely a stale invocation string.
fix: Update the README file-tree entries to the current deep/ filenames (research.md, review.md, ai-council.md) and deep/assets YAML names (deep_ai-council_auto.yaml).

title: REFUTED: phase-007 spec is a Planned scaffold, not a false consolidation claim — 5 separate trees match its documented pre-state
evidence: spec.md:41 `| **Status** | Planned (scaffold) |`; line 23 `completion_pct: 0`; line 54 Problem statement: `All five skills ship a feature_catalog and a manual_testing_playbook (roughly 198 combined playbook scenarios)`; line 59 scaffold note says plan.md/tasks.md authored when picked up; line 83 R1 `One feature_catalog root... partitioned by mode` is the TARGET. Reality (find) = 5 per-mode feature_catalog.md + 5 manual_testing_playbook.md, which the spec documents as the pre-merge state.
why: R10 reads the phase's planned TARGET as a current completion claim; the spec explicitly marks the consolidation 0%-done and describes the 5 separate trees as the starting condition — no spec-vs-reality contradiction and no structural-governance violation.
fix: No code/doc change required; R10 should be closed as refuted. Optional: track that the 198 figure is an estimate, not an enforced self-check.

title: Per-mode count self-checks hold; no broken merged total exists
evidence: improvement playbook:110 `The deep-improvement per-mode subtotal is 48 numbered scenarios (IS-001..SB-048, Lanes A/B/C)... the on-disk count is 49 scenario files`; research playbook:29 `This playbook provides 44 deterministic scenarios... (35 DR-* feature scenarios + 6 CP-* command-flow stress tests + 3 fan-out scenarios)`; rg `198` across all 10 feature_catalog/playbook files returns zero hits.
why: The merge (which would produce a 198 merged total) is unstarted, so each mode retains its own intact self-check; the spec's 198 is a forward estimate, not a live machine-enforced literal that could be broken.
fix: None; confirms the no-broken-total acceptance condition is currently satisfied for the un-consolidated trees.

title: Doctor hardcodes the `release-stable` socket namespace and ignores OD_SIDECAR_IPC_BASE
evidence: _common.sh:41 `OD_SOCKET="/tmp/open-design/ipc/release-stable/daemon.sock"`; but od_cli_reference.md:83 confirms the real scheme is `join(OD_SIDECAR_IPC_BASE | /tmp/open-design/ipc, namespace, "<app>.sock")` with namespace `release-stable` — the base is env-overridable and the namespace is channel-derived. doctor.sh:67-72 stats this exact literal.
why: Path is CONFIRMED-correct for the standard release-stable install (ref:83 `ls`-verified), so not a factual error — but a custom OD_SIDECAR_IPC_BASE or a non-`release-stable` build resolves to a different socket, making doctor.sh report `info: socket not present` (line 72) when the daemon is actually up. Non-fatal (info-level) but a false-negative diagnostic.
fix: Derive from `${OD_SIDECAR_IPC_PATH:-${OD_SIDECAR_IPC_BASE:-/tmp/open-design/ipc}/release-stable/daemon.sock}`, preferring OD_SIDECAR_IPC_PATH when exported; keep the literal as the final fallback.

title: Config-mutating `mcp install` shares one code fence with the read-only `--print --json` preview
evidence: L91-92 preview `node "$OD_BIN" mcp install opencode --print --json`; L95-96 wire `node "$OD_BIN" mcp install opencode   # deep-merges ~/.config/opencode/opencode.json (mcp.open-design)` sit in the SAME fenced block, separated only by a blank line, a `# WIRE (only after reviewing the preview):` comment, and the absence of `--print --json`.
why: A whole-block copy-paste (the common operator action) runs the mutation; the only safeguard is reading the comment and noticing one missing flag. The AI-FIRST section (L27-30) shows ONLY the preview and explicitly calls wiring 'a separate, gated step' — the safer separation pattern is not mirrored here, so an operator can conflate preview and wire.
fix: Split §4 into two fenced blocks (a read-only PREVIEW block, then a distinct WIRE block) or prefix the two wire lines with a STOP/confirm banner, matching the §0 AI-FIRST separation.

title: "never starts the daemon" guarantee for `node "$OD_BIN" --version`/`--help` is only indirectly verified
evidence: doctor.sh:3-4 asserts the script 'never starts the daemon'; doctor.sh:53,55 run `node "$OD_BIN" --version` and `node "$OD_BIN" --help`. od_cli_reference.md:141 classes bare `od` with no verb as **mutating** — 'Start the local daemon plus chat web UI'. Reference:33 tags CLI facts '[CONFIRMED ... observed by running `--help`/`--version`]', and L55-61/197-200 show `--help` as a confirmed-safe locate step — evidence LEANS SAFE (refutes the daemon-start hypothesis). No explicit `od --version` output is captured anywhere, and the host never executed this doctor.
why: The guarantee depends on the arg-parser short-circuiting `--version`/`--help` before the no-verb daemon-start default (ref:141). The reference's own investigation ran `--help`/`--version` without noting a daemon spawn, so the guarantee most likely holds; the residual is that it was never re-confirmed against THIS authored script, and INSTALL_GUIDE:77 / doctor.sh:55-58 already anticipate `--version` 'produced no output' (suggesting it is not a first-class flag).
fix: Run `node "$OD_BIN" --help` once and confirm no `/tmp/open-design/ipc/**/daemon.sock` is created and no 'Open Design Helper' process spawns; optionally drop the `--version` probe (docs note it may emit nothing) and rely on the existing `--help` fallback.
